2604.23278.title: Active Inference: A method for Phenotyping Agency in AI systems?
2604.23278.paper_summary: The proliferation of agentic artificial intelligence has outpaced the conceptual tools needed to characterize agency in computational systems. Prevailing definitions mainly rely on autonomy and goal-directedness. Here, we argue for a minimal notion open to principled inspection given three criteria: intentionality as action grounded in beliefs and desires, rationality as normatively coherent action entailed by a world model, and explainability as action causally traceable to internal states; we subsequently instantiate these as a partially observable Markov decision process under a variational framework wherein posterior beliefs, prior preferences, and the minimization of expected free energy jointly constitute an agentic action chain. Using a canonical T-maze paradigm, we evidence how empowerment, formulated as the channel capacity between actions and anticipated observations, serves as an operational metric that distinguishes zero-, intermediate-, and high-agency phenotypes through structural manipulations of the generative model. We conclude by arguing that as agents engage in epistemic foraging to resolve ambiguity, the governance controls that remain effective must shift systematically from external constraints to the internal modulation of prior preferences, offering a principled, variational bridge from computational phenotyping to AI governance strategy
2604.23278.paper_summary_zh: 代理人工智慧的擴散超越了描述計算系統中代理性的概念工具。當前的定義主要依賴於自主性和目標導向性。在此，我們主張一個開放於原則檢查的最小概念，並根據三個標準進行評估：意圖性作為基於信念和欲望的行動，理性作為由世界模型所引致的規範性一致行動，以及可解釋性作為因內部狀態而可追溯的行動；我們隨後將這些具體化為一個部分可觀察的馬可夫決策過程，在一個變分框架下，其中後驗信念、先驗偏好以及期望自由能的最小化共同構成了一個代理行動鏈。通過使用經典的T迷宮範式，我們證明了如何將賦能形式化為行動與預期觀察之間的通道容量，這作為一個操作性指標，通過生成模型的結構操作來區分零代理、中介代理和高代理表型。我們最後主張，隨著代理在認知覓食中解決模糊性，仍然有效的治理控制必須系統性地從外部約束轉向先驗偏好的內部調節，提供了一個原則性、變分的橋樑，將計算表型學與人工智慧治理策略連接起來。
2604.23278.author: Philip Wilson et.al.
2604.23278.authors: Philip Wilson, Axel Constant, Mahault Albarracin, Nicolás Hinrichs, Jasmine Moore, Daniel Polani, Karl Friston
